findings_details.title: echo ... | grep pipeline in SKILL.md will be blocked by the rq-bash-pre-dispatcher hook
findings_details.body: Line 109 runs `echo "${ARGUMENTS:-}" | grep -qE -- '--skip-pr-feedback'`. The pre-tool hook (rule-1) blocks pipelines that pipe command output for truncation/filtering. This exact pattern is blocked in live sessions (as evidenced by the blocked audit-trail commands earlier in this review). In a running Claude session the step will error with BLOCKED(rule-1) rather than executing the skip check, causing the no-rq guard to be bypassed and pr-verdict.sh to be invoked unnecessarily. Fix: replace with a parameter-expansion test, e.g. `[[ "${ARGUMENTS:-}" == *'--skip-pr-feedback'* ]]`.

findings_details.title: REPO_ROOT set after PLUGIN_DIR but PLUGIN_DIR never re-derived in the no-rq branch — missing preamble for PLUGIN_DIR in no-rq code path
findings_details.body: In Step 0.6, REPO_ROOT is derived at line 106 and used at line 107 for the no-rq check. That is fine. However when the no-rq file is absent and the skill falls through to the `else` branch, it calls `bash "$PLUGIN_DIR/pr-verdict.sh"` at line 113. PLUGIN_DIR was set at lines 103-104 in the same step, so it IS in scope within a single-agent execution. But the canonical skill-authoring invariant requires each step to be self-contained. The PLUGIN_DIR preamble at lines 103-104 does not use the canonical two-line preamble verbatim (it uses `head -1` on the first find and has no `sort -V | tail -1` fallback on the second). Check that `gate-skill-isolation.py` accepts this variant — if it does not match the canonical form it may flag the step. Currently the gate passes (skill-isolation: pass), so this is informational rather than a blocking defect, but the preamble should be verified against the canonical form in `skill-authoring.md`.

findings_details.title: printf ... | sed pipeline to extract VERDICT will be blocked by the rq-bash-pre-dispatcher hook
findings_details.body: Line 115 runs `VERDICT=$(printf '%s\n' "$VERDICT_LINE" | sed -n 's/^verdict=\([^[:space:]]*\).*/\1/p')`. This is a command pipeline using | sed, which is caught by hook rule-1 banning output truncation/filtering pipelines. Fix: use shell parameter expansion instead — `VERDICT="${VERDICT_LINE#verdict=}"; VERDICT="${VERDICT%% *}"`.

findings_details.title: pr-verdict.log written twice in non-no-rq mode: once by SKILL.md (overwrite) and once by pr-verdict.sh internal _record_proof (append)
findings_details.body: SKILL.md line 114 writes the verdict line to pr-verdict.log with `>` (overwrite). Then pr-verdict.sh's own `_record_proof` call at line 255 also writes the same verdict to the same file via `>>` (append). In non-no-rq mode the file contains the same line twice. This was true before this PR (the pre-PR code also had the same dual-write pattern) and is not made worse, but it is a latent data-quality issue. Consider removing the `printf ... > pr-verdict.log` line from SKILL.md and relying solely on pr-verdict.sh's internal _record_proof.
